questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
Dr. Erika Chavez, while acknowledging the complexity of aviary migrations, believes that technological advancements in tracking methods _______ researchers' ability to discover new migration patterns.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
questions.explanation:
For Vocabulary in Context questions, use clues from the passage to determine what type of word or phrase fits in the blank. Look for signal words that show whether the blank should be a synonym, an antonym, or a cause-and-effect relationship to another part of the sentence.
Here, it is noted that bird migrations are complex. But “while” signals that there is something that can make them less complex. Even though migrations are complex, “technological advancements” can help, or support or perhaps even enhance “researchers' ability to discover new migration patterns.”
A) “diminish” means to lessen, so this is the opposite of what you’re seeking. Eliminate A.
B) “inhibit” means prevent or hinder, so this is also the opposite of help or enhance. Eliminate B.
C) “amplify” means to make greater or larger, so this matches enhance. Keep C.
D) “ignore” means to disregard, which is not the intended meaning of the blank. Eliminate D.


This means C is the correct answer.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
In the realm of urban planning, the relationship between the city transportation commission and contractors serves as a model that _______collaborative and sustainable development. Contractors collaborate closely with transportation commission members to design transport hubs that integrate with the community to enhance the experience of the city’s residents.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
questions.explanation:
For Vocabulary in Context questions, use clues from the passage to determine what type of word or phrase fits in the blank. Look for signal words that show whether the blank should be a synonym, an antonym, or a cause-and-effect relationship to another part of the sentence.
In the first sentence, there are not too many clues as to which direction this passage is headed. In other words, the blank could either be something positive or negative about “collaboration and sustainable development.” Although the noted relationship is a “model,” it could be a model for something that is not working well. But in the second sentence the description is a positive one; close collaboration helps the city. Therefore, suitable words for this blank are exemplifies, showcases or represents.
A) “undermines” means to lessen the effectiveness or power of, so this is closer to the opposite of help or enhance. Eliminate A.
B) “contradicts” means to assert the opposite of, so is not relevant to the blank. Eliminate B.
C) “isolates” means to cause something to be alone, which is irrelevant to collaboration and sustainable development. Eliminate C.
D) “epitomizes” means to be a perfect example of, so this matches exemplifies or represents. This means D is the correct answer.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
Data scientists, in an attempt to predict future travel trends, have created models that predict future costs of airfare and _______large-scale travel interruptions due to weather. Specifically, data analyst June Chan has created a model for predicting refundable versus non-refundable airfare prices that relies on historical weather data and consumer purchasing patterns during times of the year when weather interruptions are more common.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
questions.explanation:
For Vocabulary in Context questions, use clues from the passage to determine what type of word or phrase fits in the blank. Look for signal words that show whether the blank should be a synonym, an antonym, or a cause-and-effect relationship to another part of the sentence.
Here, data scientists want to “predict future costs of airfare and” do something regarding “large-scale weather interruptions.” For the blank, reuse the word predict. This is confirmed with the creation of a “model for predicting...that relies on historical weather data.”
A) “forecast” means to predict, which is synonymous for the pre-generated answer. Keep A.
B) “cause” means to make something happen, and data scientists are not trying to make weather interruptions occur. Eliminate B.
C) “benefit” means to gain an advantage from something, and data scientists do not want to help weather interruptions. Eliminate C.
D) “adjust” means to alter. Data scientists are not trying to alter or change weather interruptions. Eliminate D.


This means A is the correct answer.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
Nutrition specialists Ricardo Alban and Vu Ngo conducted research on supplement-taking routines for individuals aged 25 – 45 and observed that, although more likely to take their supplements if taken first thing in the morning, the majority of these individuals were also more likely to do so on an empty stomach, something the specialists argue is _______ to the body’s ability to absorb fat-soluble vitamins, and that they should actually be taken with food.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
questions.explanation:
For Vocabulary in Context questions, use clues from the passage to determine what type of word or phrase fits in the blank. Look for signal words that show whether the blank should be a synonym, an antonym, or a cause-and-effect relationship to another part of the sentence.
Here, nutritionists argue that supplements “should be taken with food.” The blank is a word that describes how they feel about taking food on an empty stomach, and so they must think this is a bad idea. They might argue that it is harmful or a bad idea to take them on an empty stomach.
A) “tactful” means means sensitive in dealing with something, which does not make sense in the blank. Eliminate A.
B) “detrimental” means harmful, which matches the pre-generated answer. Keep B.
C) “essential” means necessary, and more likely nutritionists think it is necessary to take supplements with food, not on an empty stomach. Eliminate C.
D) “favorable” means advantageous. Again, nutritionists do not think it is beneficial to take supplements on an empty stomach. Eliminate D.


This means B is the correct answer.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
Bloggers have contended that social media applications, video platforms, and blogs have _______ diverse representation of voices in industries that historically excluded non-white voices by providing anybody with a computer and an internet connection the ability to create and promote their own content. Lifestyle and travel blogger Sophia provides an example of this changing landscape with her successful travel blog: her pioneering blog has brought light to the lack of Black voices in the travel industry and she is committed to using her platform to give voice to the communities she interacts with through her travel.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
questions.explanation:
For Vocabulary in Context questions, use clues from the passage to determine what type of word or phrase fits in the blank. Look for signal words that show whether the blank should be a synonym, an antonym, or a cause-and-effect relationship to another part of the sentence.
In this passage, the blank is a word that describes what “social media applications, video platforms, and blogs” have done for (or against) “diverse representation of voices.” Those voices used to be “excluded,” but there is an example of how blogs have brought to light a lack of Black voices in a particular industry. All of this points to a word like supported or highlighted.
A) “bolstered” means propped up or supported, which matches the pre-generated answer and the clues in the text. Keep A.
B) “weaken” means to lessen the impact of, which is the opposite of what the passage suggests that blogs and other social media have done for Black voices. Eliminate B.
C) “rejected” means dismiss, which is not what is argued has happened to Black voices through social media applications. Eliminate C.
D) “deceived” means lied or misled. This choice is closer to the opposite of what the blank requires. Eliminate D.


This means A is the correct answer.

questions.subject: verbal

stemSegments.text: The following text is from Frances Ellen Watkins Harper's 1896 poem “Songs for the People.”

Our world, so worn and weary,
 Needs music, pure and strong,
To hush the jangle and discords
 Of sorrow, pain, and wrong.

Music to soothe all its sorrow,
 Till war and crime shall cease;
And the hearts of men grown tender
 Girdle the world with peace.
Which choice best states the main purpose of the text?

choices: To campaign for a new national anthem.
To support the men who fight in wars.
To assert the power of music in healing from pain and hardship.
To narrate a time when a song was used to create peace.
questions.explanation:
For Main Purpose questions, it helps to come up with your own answer before looking at the choices. The correct answer should capture both the tone of the passage (often revealed by the verb at the start of the answer choice) and its central idea or purpose.
This poem describes a difficult time with “Our world, so worn and weary,” and “sorrow, pain, and wrong.” To alleviate this hardship, the author speaks of “music, pure and strong” that will “soothe all its sorrow” and “girdle the world with peace.” The main purpose is to describe how music can help in difficult times.
A) states that the purpose is to “campaign for a new national anthem.” The tone, while the tone matches slightly with campaign, a national anthem is not mentioned. Eliminate A.
B) states that the purpose is to “support” soldiers. The poem, if anything, seems to be more about using music to end war, not to “support” soldiers. Eliminate B.
C) states that the purpose is to “assert” how music is powerful in healing pain. This matches the pre-generated answer and captures the purpose of the poem. Keep C.
D) states that the purpose is “narrate a time when a song was used to create peace.” But this poem is not about one specific event. Eliminate D.


This means C is the correct answer.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
In the late 20th century, while studying butterfly diets, biologist Dr. Mariana Alonso observed that some butterfly species consumed more pollen than was previously thought. Butterflies had long been known to eat plant nectar and tree sap, but that they also consumed such large quantities of pollen was a newer finding. Upon further research, what she found was that pollen is a rich source of protein that is fundamental to the diet of many butterfly species. This led to the conclusion that the relationship between butterflies and flowers as it relates to pollination is a mutually beneficial one.

Which choice best states the main purpose of the passage?
choices: To describe Dr. Alonso’s discovery about the consumption of pollen by some butterfly species.
To celebrate butterflies for their innovative dietary sources.
To disparage previous research on what butterflies consume.
To highlight the types of flowers that attract butterflies.
questions.explanation:
For Main Purpose questions, it helps to come up with your own answer before looking at the choices. The correct answer should capture both the tone of the passage (often revealed by the verb at the start of the answer choice) and its central idea or purpose.
The passage describes something new that a biologist has observed, that “some butterfly species consumed more pollen than was previously thought.” The passage expands upon this discovery. The main purpose can be to explain a discovery made by a biologist about how much pollen some butterflies consume.
A) states that the purpose is to “describe Dr. Alonso's discovery.” The neutral tone matches the tone of the passage and the content of the passage is all about the discovery. Keep A.
B) states that the purpose is to “celebrate butterflies for their innovative dietary sources.” The passage has a more neutral tone than celebratory. Eliminate B.
C) states that the purpose is to “disparage previous research.” The word “disparage” means to criticize harshly and in a way that shows lack of respect. This is not the tone of the passage. Eliminate C.
D) states that the purpose is “highlight the types of flowers that attract butterflies.” The passage is not about any specific types of flowers. Eliminate D.


This means A is the correct answer.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
Many species of cherry blossom trees (Prunus serrulata) require a period of cold temperatures, known as chilling hours, before they can bloom in the spring. Some researchers have suggested that increased winter snowfall provides the necessary cold exposure for flowering. However, there is a lack of supporting evidence of a direct link between snowfall levels and successful cherry blossom blooming, even in years with heavy snowfall, mild winters, or unexpectedly early blooms, suggesting that _______.

Which choice most logically completes the text?
choices: cherry blossom trees bloom earlier in years with higher snowfall than in years with lower snowfall.
different cherry blossom species respond differently to fluctuations in snowfall levels.
cherry blossoms probably would not bloom at all if snowfall levels were even slightly lower.
the flowering of cherry blossoms does not depend on the amount of winter snowfall.
questions.explanation:
For Text Completion questions, first read and summarize the passage to anticipate its logical direction.
This passage begins with a hypothesis that “heavy snowfall” can provide the “cold exposure” that cherry blossoms need to flower. Then it says “however, there is a lack of supporting evidence,” which changes the direction of the passage, so the correct answer contradicts the original hypothesis. The text could be completed with something like heavy snowfall does not necessarily provide the cold exposure that cherry blossoms need to flower.
A) This choice states that the trees blossom earlier in the year in years with more snow. This supports the original hypothesis, rather than contradicting it. Eliminate A.
B) This choice states different species of cherry blossoms respond differently to changes in snowfall. Although this slightly contradicts the original hypothesis, the logical conclusion will more directly contradict it with something about the relationship between snowfall and blossoming. Eliminate B.
C) This choice states that cherry blossoms “probably would not bloom at all if snowfall levels were even slightly lower.” This choice does more to support the original hypothesis, so it is not correct. It is also important to note how extreme this choice is with “not bloom at all” and “even slightly lower.” Answers like this are frequently incorrect. Eliminate C.
D) This choice states that flowering is not dependent on snowfall. This is exactly the type of contradiction to the original hypothesis that logically completes the passage. This means D is the correct answer.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
Labor laws that outline paid break requirements for companies, are, unbeknownst to employees, often violated, says labor attorney Curtis Jones of Labor Justice for California (LJC). Jones argues that frequently the flaw lies not in the law itself, but in its not going far enough by requiring employers to explicitly inform employees of _______ right to paid breaks.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation:
For Standard English Conventions questions, use clues from the passage and the answer choices to determine which grammatical rule is being tested. Here, the answer choices in this question are possessive pronouns, so this questions tests Possessive Pronoun Reference. Pronoun Reference requires a correct pronoun in terms of both number and clarity (in what the pronoun refers back to).
To find the pronoun reference, look before and after the blank. The passage states that a certain labor law doesn't do enough because it does not require “employers to explicitly inform employees of” a “right to paid breaks.” This “right” belongs to the employees, so look for a possessive pronoun that can replace employees. Employees is third-person plural, so the corresponding possessive pronoun is their. This means D is the correct answer.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
W.E.B. Du Bois, a Black sociologist and activist, developed ideas about how systems reenforce inequity among social classes, and often along racial lines. Du Bois calls out this systemic oppression in the legal system. His claim that “laws are executed by men who have absolutely no motive for treating Black people with courtesy or _______ that those in law enforcement are not inclined to show respect for Black people when enforcing laws with them.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation:
For Standard English Conventions questions, use clues from the passage and the answer choices to determine which grammatical rule is being tested. Here, the answer choices offer different ways of joining two clauses, so this question focuses on Sentence Structure (Run-ons and Fragments).
To answer, determine whether the clause before the blank is independent or dependent. The first clause, “His claim that 'laws are executed by men who have absolutely no motive for treating Black people with courtesy or blank' ” has the subject claim but no verb, so it is a dependent clause, or not a complete sentence. The verb from the answer choices, “suggests”, completes the thought. This means the correct punctuation must connect the quotation by including suggests in the first sentence.
A)“'consideration,' suggests” correctly keeps the quotation within the same sentence. The comma indicates the sentence continues with the verb suggests. Keep A.
B)“'consideration': suggests” incorrectly uses a colon, which can only follow an independent clause. It ends the first clause off so it never gets a verb. Eliminate B.
C)“ 'consideration'; suggests” incorrectly uses a semicolon, which also joins two independent clauses. Eliminate C.
D)“'consideration.' suggests” ends the sentence too early, creating a fragment before the verb. Eliminate D.


This means the correct answer is A.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
Since 2004, the number of local print newspapers in the US has plummeted, falling by over 3,000. Some journalists _______ that the “tech boom” has not only decimated print newspapers, but also has left a huge information gap that is often filled by individuals who are not educated in traditional journalism.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation:
For Standard English Conventions questions, use clues from the passage and the answer choices to determine which grammatical rule is being tested. The answer choices here are different verb forms, so this question tests verb form.
The sentence that contains the blank has the subject “journalists,&lrquo; but does not have a verb (what are the journalists doing?). The entire passage is in present tense as shown with “has plummeted,&lrquo; “has decimated,” and “has left.” That means the correct answer is the present tense conjugation.
A)“arguing” is a gerund that would require a verb such as “started” arguing. Eliminate A.
B)“argue” is a present conjugated verb, which is what is needed in this blank. Keep B.
C)“having argued” is a verbal phrase, but it is in past tense form. Eliminate C.
D)“to argue” is the infinitive of the verb (not conjugated) and this sentence requires a conjugated verb. Eliminate D.


This means the correct answer is B.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
The laws about the sale and use of fireworks vary greatly around the world. In very few countries, fireworks can be used freely without restriction; most countries _______ the sale and use of at least the most dangerous classes of fireworks while allowing people to buy and use sparklers.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation:
For Standard English Conventions questions, use clues from the passage and the answer choices to determine which grammatical rule is being tested.The answer choices here are different verb tenses (in both conjugation and number), so this question tests verb tense and agreement.
The subject after the semi-colon is “countries.” The sentence uses the present tense (“laws vary” and “fireworks can”). This means the correct answer is the plural present tense form of the verb.
A)“prohibits” is singular and countries is a plural subject, so this is a mismatch in subject-verb agreement. Eliminate A.
B)“are prohibiting” B is a present participle conjugation and plural. It indicates something that is occurring now for a time-bound period (it will not continue in the future). This passage is about laws that are in effect around the world regarding fireworks, not something that is just happening right now. Eliminate B.
C)“have prohibited” is a past tense conjugation; it does not agree with “fireworks can.” Eliminate C.
D)“prohibit” is the plural present tense of the verb, and this is what is needed in the blank. This means D is the correct answer.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
The term “dwarf planet,” originally coined by planetary scientist Alan Stern to describe a certain type of planet, was adopted by the International Astronomical Union, which used it in a way that excludes dwarf planets as _______ the term was ultimately used to recategorize Pluto and remove its planetary status.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation:
For Standard English Conventions questions, use clues from the passage and the answer choices to determine which grammatical rule is being tested. Here, the answer choices offer different ways of joining two clauses, so this question focuses on Sentence Structure (Run-ons and Fragments).
To answer, determine whether the clauses before and after the blank are independent or dependent. The first clause (“The term `dwarf planet'…was adopted”) has a subject (term) and a verb (was), so it’s independent. The clause after the blank (“the term was widely use...”) also has both a subject (term) and a verb (was), making it another independent clause. Two independent clauses can be joined correctly with a semicolon, a comma + conjunction (for, and, nor, but, or, yet, so), or by dividing them into two sentences.
A)“planets and” includes a conjunction but no comma, so it creates in a run-on. Eliminate A.
B)“planets, and” correctly separates the two independent clauses into two complete sentences. Keep B.
C)“planets” lacks any punctuation or conjunction to connect the clauses, creating a run-on. Eliminate C.
D)“planets,” uses a comma but no conjunction, again creating a run-on. Eliminate D.


This means the correct answer is B.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
Driver ants, also known as safari ants, are organisms that rely on their large colony size and their vicious bite for their strength. Traveling in columns ranging from 20 to 50 million ants, driver ants are protected by larger, more aggressive soldier ants—specialized members of the colony who line the outer edges of the columns to form a moving barrier, _______ the swarm to travel safely over long distances and eliminate predators along the way.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation:
For Standard English Conventions questions, use clues from the passage and the answer choices to determine which grammatical rule is being tested.The answer choices here are different verb forms, so this question tests Sentence Structure and Verb Form.
The sentence up to the blank is an independent clause. That means what comes after the comma must be dependent—it can’t stand alone as a sentence, but the blank also shouldn’t create a fragment. The correct answer must connect the two parts of the sentence smoothly and logically.
A)“allows” is a singular verb, but the subject of the sentence is the plural noun soldier ants. This creates a subject-verb agreement error. Eliminate A.
B)“to allow” is an infinitive. Using an infinitive after a complete sentence creates a fragment, since it isn’t attached to a main verb or noun. Eliminate B.
C)“allowing” is a present participle. This choice forms a modifying phrase that explains the result of the ants’ action—how forming a barrier enables something else. It correctly connects the dependent phrase to the main clause. Keep C.
D)“allowed” is past tense, while the rest of the sentence is in the present tense (“line,” “create”). This creates a tense shift error. Eliminate D.


This means the correct answer is C.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
In his 1943 painting The Jungle, Cuban artist Wifredo Lam blended several art forms including Cubism, Surrealism, and Afro-Caribbean forms, creating his own unique style. Up until then, Lam’s work _______ regarded more firmly as a Surrealist painter.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation:
For Standard English Conventions questions, use clues from the passage and the answer choices to determine which grammatical rule is being tested. The answer choices here are different verb forms, so this question tests Verb Tense and Agreement.
The sentence with the blank describes something that occurred before the events described in the first sentence (“Up until then”). The correct answer must use a past tense form that fits with this time frame.
A)“would be” is present conditional. This form expresses something hypothetical or uncertain, but the passage describes a real situation that occurred in the past. Eliminate A.
B)“had been” is past perfect. This tense is used for an action that was completed before another event in the past. It correctly matches the phrase “Up until then,” which signals that his work was regarded that way before a later change. Keep B.
C)“were” is past tense but it is plural, while the subject Lam’s work is singular. This creates a subject–verb agreement error. Eliminate C.
D)“is” is present tense, but the passage clearly requires past tense. The time marker “Up until then” makes present tense incorrect. Eliminate D.


This means the correct answer is B.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
Temporary projections named pseudopods materialize and vanish on unicellular organism such as amoeba. Pseudopods are formed as extensions of the cytoplasm to aid in movement. _______ pseudopods are useful in trapping and enveloping prey.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
questions.explanation:
Logical Transition questions ask you to select the best transition word or phrase to show the relationship between two ideas, either between sentences or within a single sentence. To find the correct answer, find how the ideas connect; the second idea is usually a cause, result, contrast, or example of the first.
This passage has three sentences that describe what pseudopods are, how they form, and why they are useful. The third sentence continues the explanation by adding another detail about them.
A)“Additionally,” correctly signals that the next sentence adds more information about pseudopods. Keep A.
B)“For example,” would introduce a specific instance of pseudopods, but the sentence gives a new detail, not an example. Eliminate B.
C)“Nevertheless,” signals contrast, but the third sentence does not contrast with the previous ones. Eliminate C.
D)“In conclusion,” indicates a summary or closing idea, which does not fit here. Eliminate D.


This means the correct answer is A.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
In the 1960s, Faith Ringgold painted her first series rooted in racial and social justice. She then continued to explore different artistic media by creating sculptures, masks, and quilts. _______ Ringgold was not finished with taking on new media as she then published several books, including an autobiography and several award-winning children’s books.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
questions.explanation:
Logical Transition questions ask you to select the best transition word or phrase to show the relationship between two ideas, either between sentences or within a single sentence. To find the correct answer, find how the ideas connect; the second idea is usually a cause, result, contrast, or example of the first.
This passage is divided into two parts: the first explains that Ringgold explored various artistic media, and the second states that she was not finished expanding into new forms. The transition should therefore signal contrast, similar to “but.”
A)“As a result” suggests a cause-and-effect relationship, but the second part doesn’t result from the first. Eliminate A.
B)“However” correctly signals contrast between what she had already done and what she continued to do. Keep B.
C)“For instance” introduces an example, but the second sentence is not an example of the first — it’s a new, contrasting point. Eliminate C.
D)“Therefore” implies cause and effect, which doesn’t fit the relationship between the two ideas. Eliminate D.


This means the correct answer is B.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
Many poets write poetry that follows conventional structures pertaining to meter, rhyme, and line length. Walt Whitman’s “Song of Myself,” _______ is a free verse poem, that has zero rhymes and no fixed meter. It is this type of indifference to conventional structures that has given him the distinction of having originated free verse poetry.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
questions.explanation:
Logical Transition questions ask you to select the best transition word or phrase to show the relationship between two ideas, either between sentences or within a single sentence. To find the correct answer, find how the ideas connect; the second idea is usually a cause, result, contrast, or example of the first.
The beginning of the passage describes how many poets follow conventional poetic structures. Then the passage shifts with “Walt Whitman’s `Song of Myself,' … is a free verse poem,” contrasting with the earlier idea. Because the second sentence presents an opposing approach to traditional poetic structure, the correct transition must signal contrast.
A)“therefore,” introduces a conclusion or result, not a contrast. Eliminate A.
B)“by contrast,” correctly signals opposition between Whitman’s free verse and conventional structured poetry. Keep B.
C)“for example,” would work only if what followed were an example of a poet using conventional structure, not rejecting it. Eliminate C.
D)“in other words,” signals a restatement of the same idea, not a contrast. Eliminate D.


This means the correct answer is B.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
Historians Elena Govetti and Akbar Ebeid suggest that while tertiary sources are sometimes overlooked, they can actually offer a more objective lens of a particular event or topic. _______ Govetti and Ebeid appear to be in the minority in this line of thinking as most historians maintain that tertiary sources are equally subject to the bias of the historical time period from which they come.is a free verse poem, that has zero rhymes and no fixed meter.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
questions.explanation:
Logical Transition questions ask you to select the best transition word or phrase to show the relationship between two ideas, either between sentences or within a single sentence. To find the correct answer, find how the ideas connect; the second idea is usually a cause, result, contrast, or example of the first.
The passage introduces a belief that some historians, Govetti and Ebeid, hold about tertiary sources. The next sentence reveals that their view is not widely shared, so the relationship between the two ideas is one of contrast. The correct transition should function like “but.”
A)“However,” correctly signals contrast between the minority view of Govetti and Ebeid and the majority view of other historians. Keep A.
B)“Due to this,” indicates a cause-and-effect relationship, which does not fit the contrast between these ideas. Eliminate B.
C)“Decreasingly,” means that something is happening less often, which is unrelated to the relationship between the sentences. Eliminate C.
D)“Equally important,” introduces an additional point of similar weight, not a contrasting idea. Eliminate D.


This means the correct answer is A.

questions.subject: verbal

stemSegments.text: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

• Dinosaur Park is a roadside attraction in Rapid City, South Dakota.
• It has seven of the world's largest model dinosaurs.
• The largest dinosaur weighs approximately 185,000 pounds.
• Cabazon Dinosaurs is a roadside attraction in Cabazon, California.
• It has two of the largest model dinosaurs in the world.
• The largest dinosaur weighs approximately 300,000 pounds.
The student wants to compare the weights of two of the dinosaurs at the respective parks. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

choices: Some of the model dinosaurs in the United States, including one of the two at Cabazon Dinosaurs, weigh more than \(\text{150,000}\) pounds.
The largest dinosaur at Dinosaur Park weighs about \(\text{185,000}\) pounds, while the significantly heavier dinosaur at Cabazon Dinosaurs weighs about \(\text{300,000}\) pounds.
The largest dinosaur at Dinosaur Park, which boasts seven of the largest model dinosaurs in the world, weighs approximately \(\text{185,000}\).
Dinosaur Park, located in South Dakota, and Cabazon Dinosaurs, located in California, are examples of roadside attractions.
questions.explanation:
For Student Research questions, focus on what the student wants to highlight or accomplish. In this case, the student wants to compare the weights of two of the dinosaurs at the respective parks. Therefore, the correct answer must mention both parks and provide a clear comparison of the dinosaurs’ weights.
Looking at the list of bulleted notes, the following provide the relevant information:
  • The largest dinosaur at Dinosaur Park weighs approximately 185,000 pounds.
  • The largest dinosaur at Cabazon Dinosaurs weighs approximately 300,000 pounds.
The correct answer must paraphrase these notes and highlight the difference in weight between the two dinosaurs.
A)This choice mentions only one park and gives an approximate weight range, not a comparison between the two dinosaurs. Eliminate A.
B)This choice directly compares the two dinosaurs, mentioning both parks and showing that the Cabazon dinosaur is significantly heavier. Keep B.
C)This choice discusses only Dinosaur Park and omits any mention of the Cabazon Dinosaurs, so it fails to make a comparison. Eliminate C.
D)This choice mentions both parks but does not include any information about the weights of their dinosaurs, which is the focus of the question. Eliminate D.


This means the correct answer is B.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
When analyzing presidential speeches, historians frequently highlight an orator’s ability to _______ deliver a speech in order to inspire and garner the support of the masses. Abraham Lincoln is known for this skill as his speeches and his delivery were clear, expressive, and powerful.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
questions.explanation:
For Vocabulary in Context questions, use clues from the passage to determine what type of word or phrase fits in the blank. Look for signal words that show whether the blank should be a synonym, an antonym, or a cause-and-effect relationship to another part of the sentence.
Here, the word in the blank describes how presidents deliver speeches “to inspire and garner the support of the masses.” The next sentence gives more clues, describing Lincoln’s speeches as “clear, expressive, and powerful.” Therefore, the correct word should mean something like clearly or expressively.
A) “disparagingly” means in a disrespectful way, contradicting the positive tone of the passage. Eliminate A.
B) “eloquently” means in a fluent, persuasive, and expressive way, which matches the clues “clear, expressive, and powerful.” Keep B.
C) “incompletely” means lacking, which doesn’t describe effective speech delivery. Eliminate C.
D) “emphatically” means forcefully or with emphasis. While somewhat positive, it doesn’t capture the nuance of clarity and expressiveness emphasized in the passage. Eliminate D.


This means B is the correct answer.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
As the vast quantity of evidence mounts from a wide variety of sources in favor of nationwide education reform, it is a surprise that national legislation has entertained _______ number of modifications to education policy. State legislators and the education secretary will seek to highlight this disparity at the next open sessions on education reform.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
questions.explanation:
For Vocabulary in Context questions, use clues from the passage to determine what type of word or phrase fits in the blank. Look for signal words that show whether the blank should be a synonym, an antonym, or a cause-and-effect relationship to another part of the sentence.
Here, the passage states that there is “a vast quantity of evidence” supporting nationwide education reform, but follows this with “it is a surprise that national legislation has entertained … number of modifications.” The phrase “it is a surprise” signals contrast, suggesting that the number of modifications must be smaller than expected. Later, the passage refers to this as a “disparity,” reinforcing the idea of an unexpectedly small number of changes.
A) “paltry” means small, and fits perfectly with the context of surprisingly few modifications. Keep A.
B) “sizeable” means large or significant, the opposite of what the passage suggests. Eliminate B.
C) “unspecified” means undefined or not stated, which doesn’t relate to the surprising smallness described. Eliminate C.
D) “`indecisive” means uncertain or hesitant, which describes a person’s behavior, not a number or quantity. Eliminate D.


This means A is the correct answer.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
Bell Labs research done in the late 1950s resulted in the Picturephone, which was created by AT\&T in 1964 as the first video phone, and was showcased at a World’s Fair and in several cities across the US. However, the Picturephone was not the _______ invention that the video phone is now; today video calling is a highly respected innovation, one that is fundamental to the work and personal lives of millions.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
questions.explanation:
For Vocabulary in Context questions, use clues from the passage to determine what type of word or phrase fits in the blank. Look for signal words that show whether the blank should be a synonym, an antonym, or a cause-and-effect relationship to another part of the sentence.
Here, the signal word “However” marks a contrast between how the Picturephone was viewed in its time and how the video phone is regarded now. The phrase that the Picturephone “was not the invention” already sets up a negative structure, so the word in the blank should describe something positive—a respected or admired invention—so that the overall meaning becomes a reversal: the Picturephone was not the admired or celebrated invention that the modern video phone has become.
A) “observed” means merely seen or noted; it doesn’t pair naturally with invention' in this construction (an observed invention) and doesn’t capture the contrast between the Picturephone and the video phone. Eliminate A.
B) “speculated” means theorized or guessed; a speculated invention' is not idiomatic here and doesn’t fit the contrast in reputation. Eliminate B.
C) “failed” would make the phrase not the “failed invention,” which is too strong—nothing in the passage claims the Picturephone failed. Eliminate C.
D) “esteemed” means respected/admired. Saying it was not the esteemed invention that the video phone is now cleanly sets up the contrast with “highly respected innovation.” This means D is the correct answer.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
Research conducted by a team of graduate students at an Australian university found that increasing the time between when a person last looks at their phone and the time when they go to bed can lead to increased sleep quality. The team administered an experiment with a group of 500 individuals, half of whom were instructed to continue to use their devices as they normally would, the other half of whom were instructed to shut off all electronic devices exactly three hours before their bedtime. All participants responded to a daily survey that used a series of qualitative and quantitative metrics to calculate sleep quality. The team found that the group who shut down their electronic devices three hours before going to bed reported, on average, a measured sleep quality that was \(40\%\) higher than the average sleep quality of the control group.

Which choice best states the function of the underlined sentence in the overall structure of the text?
choices: To summarize the findings of the team after conducting the experiment.
To expand upon the methodology used by the team in the experiment.
To introduce an alternative experiment conducted by the team.
To undermine the methodology used by the team in the experiment.
questions.explanation:
For Sentence Function questions, it helps to read the sentence before and after the underlined sentence. Identify what the underlined sentence does in relation to the previous sentence (i.e. introduces a new idea, supports a claim, contrasts with earlier information, etc.).
This text explains the purpose and setup of a research study about the relationship between phone use and sleep quality. The underlined sentence, “All participants responded to a daily survey that used a series of qualitative and quantitative metrics to calculate sleep quality,” gives a specific detail about how the researchers measured results. Therefore, its function is to describe one of the steps or methods used in the experiment.
A) This choice says the sentence “summarizes the findings of the team after conducting the experiment.” The sentence does not summarize findings—it explains the data collection method. Eliminate A.
B) This choice says the sentence “expands upon the methodology used by the team in the experiment.” This fits perfectly, since it adds detail about how sleep quality was measured. Keep B.
C) This choice says the sentence “introduces an alternative experiment conducted by the team.” The text only discusses one experiment, so this is incorrect. Eliminate C.
D) This choice says the sentence “undermines the methodology used by the team in the experiment.” There is no suggestion of criticism here. Eliminate D.


This means B is the correct answer.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
Sterlin Harjo was born in 1979 and grew up in Oklahoma. A Seminole Nation of Oklahoma citizen with Muscogee heritage as well, Harjo is a pre-eminent filmmaker, particularly of films representing Native American people. In a paper for an indigenous peoples’ rights class, a student asserts that Harjo’s films and shows upend stereotypical representations of Native people and provide an authentic lens, one that resonates with the experiences of many Native people.

Which finding, if true, would most directly support the student’s claim?
choices: Harjo hires a diverse filmmaking crew of various ethnicities and background, including writers and actors who represent many perspectives of Native people.
Harjo mimics the way Native Americans have always been portrayed in Hollywood to draw inspiration for his own films.
Harjo’s entire regular cast and crew, including writers, consists of Native people who drive genuine storytelling of Native people.
Harjo only films in Oklahoma in order to authentically represent the Seminole Nation of Oklahoma.
questions.explanation:
For Supporting or Weakening Claim questions, first identify the claim. This passage discusses a filmmaker and his impact on how Native Americans are portrayed in film and television. The student claims that Harjo has completely changed the “stereotypical” way Native Americans were portrayed, and that many Native peoples connect with his films. The correct answer will include evidence that supports Harjo’s authentic and thorough representation of Native people.
A) states that Harjo hires a diverse filmmaking crew that includes writers and actors who represent many Native perspectives. While this partially supports the claim by showing some authentic Native involvement, it doesn’t directly establish that Harjo’s films fully upend stereotypes. Eliminate A.
B) states that Harjo mimics the way Native Americans have always been portrayed in Hollywood. This contradicts the student’s claim—if Harjo were imitating those portrayals, his films would reinforce stereotypes rather than challenge them. Eliminate B.
C) states that Harjo’s entire regular cast and crew, including writers, are Native people who drive genuine storytelling. This directly supports the student’s claim that Harjo’s work provides an authentic lens reflecting Native experiences, as it shows that Native people themselves shape the narratives. Keep C.
D) states that Harjo only films in Oklahoma to represent the Seminole Nation authentically. While this might show some cultural grounding, it’s too narrow; the student’s claim is about broader Native representation, not just one tribe or region. Eliminate D.


This means C is the correct answer.

questions.subject: verbal

stemSegments.text: The following text is from Ernest Hemingway’s 1940 novel For Whom the Bell Tolls. Robert Jordan is an American fighting against the Fascists in Spain during the Spanish Civil War.

He had not liked Gaylord's, the hotel in Madrid the Russians had taken over, when he first went there because it seemed too luxurious and the food was too good for a besieged city and the talk too cynical for a war. But I corrupted very easily, he thought. Why should you not have as good food as could be organized when you came back from something like this?
Which choice best states the main purpose of the text?

choices: To explain Jordan’s changing feelings about enjoying oneself and good things during a war.
To cast doubt on Jordan’s commitment to the war effort.
To describe Jordan’s disgust that people can enjoy luxuries during a war.
To highlight Jordan’s love of luxurious living.
questions.explanation:
For Main Purpose questions, it helps to come up with your own answer before looking at the choices. The correct answer should capture both the tone of the passage (often revealed by the verb at the start of the answer choice) and its central idea or purpose.
In this passage, Jordan had an idea about Gaylord's that it was too luxurious for wartime, but then he had a change of heart and felt that he should be able to enjoy “good food” during wartime. The main purpose, therefore, is to describe that Jordan changed his mind and came to think it's okay to enjoy luxuries even during the war. At first, he judges Gaylord’s hotel as inappropriate for a war setting, but later he accepts that it’s reasonable to enjoy good food and comfort after hardship.
A) The verb explain fits the reflective, introspective tone of the passage, and this choice directly captures Jordan’s changing feelings about enjoying luxuries during war. Keep A.
B) The verb cast doubt implies that the passage questions Jordan’s loyalty to the war effort, but nothing in the text suggests his dedication is wavering. Eliminate B.
C) The verb describe is neutral, but this choice focuses only on Jordan’s initial disgust, ignoring his later change of heart. Eliminate C.
D) The phrase highlight Jordan’s love of luxurious living misrepresents the passage; Jordan doesn’t love luxury. He simply comes to accept it as okay to enjoy even during wartime. Eliminate D.


This means A is the correct answer.

questions.subject: verbal

stemSegments.text: The following text is from Richard Lovelace’s 1642 poem “To Althea, from Prison.”  The poem was written while Lovelace was imprisoned and was written as a letter to a woman named Althea.

Stone Walls do not a Prison make,
Nor Iron bars a Cage;
Minds innocent and quiet take,
That for an Hermitage.

If I have freedom in my Love,
And in my soul am free,
Angels alone that soar above,
Enjoy such Liberty.
What is the main idea of the text?

choices: The speaker feels trapped inside the prison of his mind and the actual prison.
The speaker believes that if he feels free in his mind he cannot be confined by prison.
The speaker feels he has justly served out his sentence and should be freed from the physical prison.
The speaker believes that it is just that he is imprisoned for his wrongdoing.
questions.explanation:
For Main Idea questions, the correct answer will capture the central concept that connects all parts of the passage, not just one example or detail.
Here, the speaker, writing from prison, claims that the physical prison is not what creates imprisonment with “Stone Walls do not a Prison make, Nor Iron bars a Cage.” It explains that with love, he is actually free even though he is in prison. The central idea, then, is that spiritual or emotional freedom cannot be taken away by physical imprisonment.
A) states that the speaker feels trapped both physically and mentally. This directly contradicts the poem, where the speaker emphasizes his inner freedom despite being in prison. Eliminate A.
B) states that the speaker believes that if he feels free in his mind, he cannot be confined by prison. This exactly matches the main idea of the poem: that freedom of the mind and soul transcends physical barriers. Keep B.
C) states that the speaker feels he has served his sentence and should be freed. There is no mention of his sentence length or a plea for release. Eliminate C.
D) states that the speaker believes he deserves imprisonment. The poem makes no reference to guilt or wrongdoing. Eliminate D.


This means B is the correct answer.

questions.subject: verbal

stemSegments.text: The following text is from Charles Dicken’s 1859 novel A Tale of Two Cities.

In England, there was scarcely an amount of order and protection to justify much national boasting.  Daring burglaries by armed men, and highway robberies, took place in the capital itself every night; families were publicly cautioned not to go out of town without removing their furniture to upholsterers’ warehouses for security…thieves snipped off diamond crosses from the necks of noble lords at Court drawing-rooms.
According to the text, what is true about England at this time?

choices: It was a period when the people of England could be proud of their country.
Families never went out of town without relocating their furniture.
There were robberies in the city capital every night.
questions.explanation:
For According to the Text questions, the correct answer will be directly supported by the passage, though often expressed in slightly different wording. Here, the passage describes England during a particular period. Use process of elimination to find the statement is true about England during that time.
A) states that it was a period when the people of England could be proud of their country. The passage contradicts this in the first sentence, which states there was “scarcely an amount of order and protection to justify much national boasting.” Eliminate A.
B) says that families never went out of town without relocating their furniture. The passage only says that families were “publicly cautioned” to do this, but that they never did is too strong. Eliminate B.
C) says that there were robberies in the city capital every night. Although every is strong language requiring strong evidence, the passage does directly support this, stating that “highway robberies took place in the capital itself every night.” Keep C.
D) says that nobody was proud of England. Like B, this uses overly strong wording. The passage says there wasn’t much reason for boasting, but it doesn’t say that nobody felt pride. Eliminate D.


This means C is the correct answer.

questions.subject: verbal

stemSegments.text: Highest Education Level (in millions) for California, Massachusetts, Minnesota, and Virginia

StateTotal Population of High School Graduates or EquivalentTotal Population with Bachelor's or Graduate/Professional Degrees
Massachusetts1.101.3 (bachelor's), 1.1 (graduate or professional)
Virginia1.451.4 (bachelor's), 1.1 (graduate or professional)
California5.506.1 (bachelor's), 4 (graduate or professional)
Minnesota0.901 (bachelor's), 0.8 (graduate or professional)

Recent census data on highest education levels attained by populations in the United States indicate that certain states have similar population sizes for high school (or equivalent) graduates as they do for bachelor's and graduate degrees. Since these figures include people born in other states, some of the data for bachelor's and graduate degrees might be inflated whereas the data for high school graduates might accurately represent the state population; it's plausible, for example, that _______
Which choice most effectively uses data from the table to complete the example?

choices: In Virgina and California, approximately one-half of high school graduates were born out of state.

In Massachusetts and Virginia most of those whose highest education level was high school were born in-state while far more than half of those with graduate or professional degrees were born in other states.
Minnesota has more than 2 million high school graduates who were born in other states.
Most of those with bachelor’s or graduate degrees in California as their highest education level were born in California.
questions.explanation:
To answer a Quantitative Information question, identify what the statement says and find the corresponding data in the table that either matches or completes the statement. Here, the passage discusses census data suggesting that the number of people with bachelor’s and graduate degrees in some states may be inflated because many of those individuals were born in other states, while the data for high school graduates more accurately reflects those born in-state. The correct answer should provide an example that fits this explanation.
A)says that about half of high school graduates were born out of state in Virginia and California. This contradicts the passage’s premise that high school data reflects mostly in-state residents. Eliminate A.
B)says that in Massachusetts and Virginia, most people whose highest education level is high school were born in-state, while more than half of those with bachelor’s or graduate degrees were born in other states. This matches the hypothesis that the higher education data might be inflated due to migration, while the high school data is more locally accurate. Keep B.
C)says that Minnesota has more than two million high school graduates who were born in other states. The table shows only \(0.9\) million total high school graduates in Minnesota, making this factually incorrect. Eliminate C.
D)says that most of those with bachelor’s or graduate degrees in California were born in California. This contradicts the example’s logic that these higher degree populations may be inflated by people born elsewhere. Eliminate D.


This means the correct answer is B.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
Written in 1215 to help solve the problems between King John of England and the barons, the Magna Carta was a peace agreement that set out to establish rights for free men. _______ influence since then has been seen in other civil liberties documents, including the US Bill of Rights written almost 600 years later.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation:
For Standard English Conventions questions, use clues from the passage and the answer choices to determine which grammatical rule is being tested. Here, the answer choices are different types of pronouns, so this question tests Possessive Pronoun Reference. Pronoun reference requires that the pronoun correctly matches the noun it refers to in both number (singular or plural) and clarity (what the pronoun refers back to).
The blank followed by “influence” begs the question “whose influence,” and since the answers include possessive pronouns, one of them must go in the blank. Eliminate A and B because they are not possessive pronouns (they are contractions of “they are” and “it is”, respectively). The first sentence is about the Magna Carta and what it was designed to do. The sentence then ends with “free men,” so it can seem unclear whether the possessive pronoun should replace Magna Carta (singular) or free men (plural). The second sentence talks about the “influence” on other documents, so it must be in reference to the Magna Carta, which is also a document. The sentence could read “The Magna Carta's influence,” but it would not make sense for the sentence to read “The free men's influence” because the men did not influence the other documents.
C)“Their” is plural, but the noun it refers to (the Magna Carta) is singular. Eliminate C.
D)“Its” is singular and possessive, and correctly refers to the Magna Carta. This means the correct answer is D.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
Biologists observe that, unlike many other species of trees, the dragon blood tree (Dracaena cinnabari) retains moisture with its huge umbrella-shaped top that the tree uses to protect _______ from the hot sun, allowing it to survive in hot and dry environments.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation:
For Standard English Conventions questions, use clues from the passage and the answer choices to determine which grammatical rule is being tested. Here, the answer choices are different kinds of pronouns, so this question tests Pronoun Reference and Agreement.
The sentence describes how the dragon blood tree uses its umbrella-shaped top to “protect” something “from the hot sun.” The subject of the sentence is the tree, and the action (uses) is performed by the tree. Because the subject and the object of the action are the same, the sentence requires a reflexive pronoun.
A)“its” is possessive, which would mean the tree is protecting something that belongs to it, not itself. Eliminate A.
B)“itself” is the reflexive pronoun that refers back to a singular subject, matching the singular noun tree. Keep B.
C)“them” is plural not reflexive, so it doesn’t agree in number or function. Eliminate C.
D)“themselves” is also plural and doesn’t agree with the singular subject. Eliminate D.


This means the correct answer is B.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
In the early 1800s, the United Kingdom, Spain, Portugal, and France were entangled in what became known as the Peninsular War. Spain formed an alliance with France to invade Portugal. While at first allied with France, upon realizing that this alliance was simply a plot to control it, _______ engaging in a war that ultimately resulted in the Napolean’s downfall.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
choices: Spain took up arms against France,
the French military was fought by Spain
Spain’s military took up arms against the French military
it was Spain who took up arms again the French military,
questions.explanation:
For Standard English Conventions questions, use clues from the passage and the answer choices to determine which grammatical rule is being tested. Here, the sentence begins with introductory modifying phrases (“While at first allied with France” and “upon realizing that this alliance was simply a plot to control it”), so this question tests Sentence Structure (Introductory Modifiers).
A modifying phrase at the start of a sentence must be followed immediately by the noun it modifies. In this case, the phrase “While at first allied with France” means that what comes next must answer the question who was allied with France?. The previous sentence states that “Spain formed an alliance with France,” so the clause after the comma must begin with Spain.
A) “Spain took up arms against France” correctly identifies the noun being modified (Spain) and provides a clear, complete clause. Keep A.
B)“the French military was fought by Spain” is grammatically awkward and shifts focus away from the modifier; it also creates passive voice unnecessarily. Eliminate B.
C)“Spain’s military took up arms against the French military” is wordy and inaccurate—the modifier describes Spain as a nation, not specifically its military. Eliminate C.
D)“it was Spain who took up arms against the French military” is ungrammatical because the subject after the modifier must come directly, not be delayed by “it was.” Eliminate D.


This means A is the correct answer.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
With all of her collections of paintings up to and including New York Hour (2004), artist Sarah Keil had painted only industrial and urban scenes. The highly acclaimed collection was Keil’s last of its kind, and her subsequent collections _______ in sharp contrast. In 2009, she painted a collection, Retreat, which featured only paintings of rural and natural scenes.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation:
For Standard English Conventions questions, use clues from the passage and the answer choices to determine which grammatical rule is being tested. Here, the answer choices show different verb tenses and singular/plural forms, so this question tests Verb Tense and Agreement.
First, identify the subject of the verb. The subject is “collections,” which is plural. This means the correct verb must also be plural, so eliminate singular options A (“was”) and C (“has been”).
Next, determine the correct tense. The passage’s first sentence uses past perfect (“had painted”), describing actions completed in the past. The following sentences also refer to past events (“The highly acclaimed collection was Keil’s last of its kind,” “In 2009, she painted…”), which means the verb in the blank should continue that past timeline.
B)“have been” is plural and consistent with the ongoing past-to-present context. It connects the past (her earlier collections) with later changes in her work. Keep B.
D)“is” is present tense, and doesn’t fit the given sequence of past events. Eliminate D.


This means the correct answer is B.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
Many states in the United States passed their first all-encompassing seat belt laws in the 1990s, requiring all passengers to wear seat belts, and the authorization of police officers to pull drivers over if anybody in their car was in violation of the seatbelt requirement was an important part of these new _______ previous legislation, only front seat passengers and the driver were required to wear seat belts.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation:
For Standard English Conventions questions, use clues from the passage and the answer choices to determine which grammatical rule is being tested. Here, the answer choices test different ways to connect or separate two clauses, so this question focuses on Sentence Structure (Run-ons and Fragments).
The sentence up to the blank, “the authorization of police officers to pull drivers over…was an important part of these new regulations,” is an independent clause (it has both a subject and a verb). The part after the blank, “under previous legislation, only front seat passengers and the driver were required to wear seat belts,” is also an independent clause.
A)“regulations under” incorrectly suggests that the new laws were passed under old laws, which changes the meaning. Eliminate A.
B)“regulations, under” connects two independent clauses with just a comma, creating a fragment after it. Eliminate B.
C)“regulations. Under” correctly separates the two independent clauses into two complete sentences, fixing the run-on error. Keep C.
D)“regulations and under” creates an ungrammatical construction where the meaning is that seatbelt regulations are an important part of the new and previous legislation. Eliminate D.


This means the correct answer is C.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
To remove the naturally occurring element arsenic from drinking water, chemist Tsanangurayi Tongesayi has developed an affordable method for using tiny plastic fragments from water bottles that are covered with an amino acid, cysteine. The plastic fragments are added to the drinking water and _______ to the arsenic to make the water safe to drink.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation:
For Standard English Conventions questions, use clues from the passage and the answer choices to determine which grammatical rule is being tested. Here, the answer choices differ in verb form and tense, so this question tests Parallel Structure and Verb Agreement}.
The sentence contains a parallel construction describing what the plastic fragments do. They “are added” and they “something to the arsenic.” Since the first verb in the list is plural and present tense (“are added”), the second verb must also be plural and present tense to maintain parallelism.
A)“binds” is singular, which disagrees with the plural subject fragments. Eliminate A.
B)“bind” is plural present tense, matching the subject and with correct parallel structure. Keep B.
C)“binding” is a participle that modifies a noun or verb, but it leaves the sentence without a main verb. Eliminate C.
D)“bound” is a past tense or past participle form, which does not fit the present tense construction of the rest of the sentence. Eliminate D.


This means the correct answer is B.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
During World War I, Canada suffered large numbers of casualties as they fought as allies to England. At the outset of this war, British rule still dictated Canadian foreign policy, and so Canada entered the war before the United States or Mexico. _______, Canada, still a loyal British ally, was the first North American country to enter the Second World War.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
questions.explanation:
Logical Transition questions ask you to select the best transition word or phrase to show the relationship between two ideas, either between sentences or within a single sentence. To find the correct answer, find how the ideas connect; the second idea is usually a cause, result, contrast, or example of the first.
Before the blank, the passage describes how Canada joined World War I early because of its alliance with England. After the blank, the passage says that Canada again joined World War II early for the same reason, its ongoing loyalty to Britain. These ideas are parallel, showing a similar situation occurring twice.
A)“Furthermore” means in addition, which could work but doesn’t emphasize the similarity between the two events as clearly. Eliminate A.
B)“However” introduces a contrast, but the two sentences are parallel, not contrasting. Eliminate B.
C)“Consequently” signals cause and effect, which doesn’t fit since Canada’s involvement in WWII was not a direct result of its WWI involvement. Eliminate C.
D)“Likewise” means in a similar way, and perfectly connects the two ideas. Canada entered both wars early because of its alliance with England. This means the correct answer is D.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
In the Roman Empire, several Roman leaders practiced Mithridatism, a routine in which they consumed small doses of poison to build immunity, in an attempt to avoid death by poisoning. Mithriditism, _______ was guided by Roman doctors who worked to improve the formulas and by religious leaders who added a religious component to the practice.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
questions.explanation:
Logical Transition questions ask you to select the best transition word or phrase to show the relationship between two ideas, either between sentences or within a single sentence. To find the correct answer, find how the ideas connect; the second idea is usually a cause, result, contrast, or example of the first.
Before the blank, the passage describes the practice of “Mithridatism”, that is, consuming small doses of poison to build immunity. After the blank, the passage explains how this practice was guided by Roman doctors and religious leaders, adding more detail about how Mithridatism was carried out.
A)“moreover,” means in addition to, or indicates that the next idea builds on the previous one. This matches the relationship between the two parts of the passage. Keep A.
B)“for example,” introduces an illustration or instance, but the second sentence doesn’t give an example of Mithridatism; it describes how the practice worked. Eliminate B.
C)“on the other hand,” sets up a contrast, and the two ideas are not contrasting. Eliminate C.
D)“therefore,” indicates a cause-and-effect relationship, but the second sentence does not result from the first; it adds further information. Eliminate D.


This means the correct answer is A.

questions.subject: verbal

stemSegments.text: While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

• Until WWI, most battles had taken place on the ground, but WWI saw the advent of airplanes in wars.
• In 1911, leaders met to make a law that countries could only use airplanes for “reconnaissance,” which is gathering information about enemies from the air. 
• One example of successful reconnaissance was when a British pilot saw the German military was going to invade France, and this information helped France avoid devastation. 
• Airplanes started to be used for more than just reconnaissance and in 1915, the Germans made a plane that was purposely designed with machine guns to fight air battles to take down enemy planes. 
• By the end of WWI, aircraft were more commonly used as weapons, bombing enemy supplies and soldiers on the ground.

The student wants to highlight an example of a turning point when aircraft went from being used to collect information to when they started to be used as weapons. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

choices: A British pilot averted Germany’s invasion of France when he saw that Germany was preparing to invade.
The end of WWI saw the use of aircraft to bomb ground forces.
The Germans designed a plane to fight against other planes in the air.
A law was written to restrict the use of aircraft as weapons, limiting them to use for collecting information about enemies.
questions.explanation:
For Student Research questions, focus on what the student wants to highlight or accomplish. In this case, the student wants to identify the turning point when aircraft went from being used for reconnaissance to being used as weapons. The correct answer should highlight the specific moment this transition occurred.
Looking at the list of bulleted notes, one stands out:
  • Airplanes started to be used for more than just reconnaissance, and in 1915, the Germans made a plane that was purposely designed with machine guns to fight air battles to take down enemy planes.
This note clearly marks the shift from aircraft being used to gather information to being used as weapons.
A)describes a successful reconnaissance mission, which shows aircraft being used for information-gathering, not the shift away from it. Eliminate A.
B)states that by the end of WWI, aircraft were used to bomb ground forces. Although this shows aircraft as weapons, it does not pinpoint the turning point when this change began. Eliminate B.
C)describes the exact moment when airplanes began to be used as weapons—when the Germans created a plane with machine guns for air battles. This best matches the student’s goal. Keep C.
D)discusses a law that restricted aircraft use to reconnaissance. This describes an earlier stage before the turning point, so eliminate D.


This means the correct answer is C.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
In his groundbreaking research, Professor Ghanem challenged conventional beliefs and paved the way for a more nuanced understanding of the creative process. Through his research, he sought to _______ a long-held misconception about the correlation between creativity and chaos.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
questions.explanation:
For Vocabulary in Context questions, use clues from the passage to determine what type of word or phrase fits in the blank. Look for signal words that show whether the blank should be a synonym, an antonym, or a cause-and-effect relationship to another part of the sentence.
Here, the passage says that Professor Ghanem “challenged conventional beliefs” and sought to correct “a long-held misconception.” These clues indicate that his goal was to contradict, or disprove an existing idea.
A) “debunk” means to expose or disprove something false, which fits perfectly with the idea of challenging a misconception. Keep A.
B) “validate” means to confirm or support, which is the opposite of what the professor was doing. Eliminate B.
C) “overlook” means to ignore or fail to notice, which doesn’t make sense in the context of actively challenging beliefs. Eliminate C.
D) “trivialize” means to make something seem less important, which doesn’t fit as well as debunk’’ because the professor wasn’t minimizing the misconception—he was disproving it. Eliminate D.


This means A is the correct answer.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
The method used by many species of spiders to attract their prey is a classic example of visual trickery in nature. It has been observed that spiders spin their webs to _______ flowers that their insect prey typically feed off of, and this attracts and traps these insects into their predator’s webs.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
questions.explanation:
For Vocabulary in Context questions, use clues from the passage to determine what type of word or phrase fits in the blank. Look for signal words that show whether the blank should be a synonym, an antonym, or a cause-and-effect relationship to another part of the sentence.
Here, the passage describes spiders using “visual trickery” to lure their prey. The sentence explains that they spin webs in a way that attracts their prey, suggesting they make their webs look like flowers, so the blank is something like make them look like or mimic flowers.
A) “eradicate” means to destroy completely, which makes no sense in this context. Eliminate A.
B) “emulate” means to imitate or reproduce something, which perfectly fits the idea of spiders spinning webs to resemble flowers. Keep B.
C) “outwit” means to trick or outsmart. While spiders do trick their prey, the blank refers to the webs’ relationship to the flowers, not the act of tricking itself. Eliminate C.
D) “coexist” means to exist together at the same time, which doesn’t fit the meaning of the sentence. Eliminate D.


This means B is the correct answer.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
In his latest memoir, Jack Thomsin seeks to _______ the inevitable critique of attributing his success in film to nepotism and good luck: he thwarts this view by littering the book with stories of rejection and childhood poverty and claims that he changed his name to avoid recognition of his famous family name.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
questions.explanation:
For Vocabulary in Context questions, use clues from the passage to determine what type of word or phrase fits in the blank. Look for signal words that show whether the blank should be a synonym, an antonym, or a cause-and-effect relationship to another part of the sentence.
Here, an author is criticized for gaining success due to “nepotism” and “good luck” (nepotism means favoritism due to family or other connections). He “thwarts this view,” meaning he attempts to oppose this perspective through his writing about his hard life. Going back to the blank early in the passage, the author seeks to thwart or oppose the criticism.
A) “vilify” means to criticize or speak ill of someone, which doesn’t fit because Thomsin isn’t attacking anyone. He’s defending himself. Eliminate A.
B) “extol” means to praise, which is the opposite of what’s needed. Eliminate B.
C) “endorse” means to support or approve, which also contradicts the idea of opposing the critique. Eliminate C.
D) “forestall” means to prevent something before it happens. This fits perfectly, since Thomsin is trying to preempt or fend off the criticism through his memoir. This means D is the correct answer.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.stem:
As one of the only male actors of his time to be cast in Black roles, Sidney Poitier was _______ about some of these roles. One the one hand, he appreciated that many of the roles challenged long-held stereotypes held about Black people, but on the other hand these roles often went too far in the other direction, portraying characters that had to be without flaws and who were portrayed in an overly idealized manner.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
questions.explanation:
For Vocabulary in Context questions, use clues from the passage to determine what type of word or phrase fits in the blank. Look for signal words that show whether the blank should be a synonym, an antonym, or a cause-and-effect relationship to another part of the sentence.
The blank describes how Poitier “was” or felt about some of his movie roles. Looking for clues as to how he felt, it says “On the one hand...” and “on the other hand,” so Poitier has mixed feelings about some of the roles he was given. Poitier both appreciated and criticized the roles he was given. These opposing views suggest he had mixed feelings.
A) “ambivalent” means having mixed or contradictory feelings, which fits the context exactly. Keep A.
B) “elated” means extremely happy, which doesn’t fit because Poitier wasn’t purely positive about the roles. Eliminate B.
C) “aggrieved” means resentful or angry, which is too negative to describe his balanced, conflicted feelings. Eliminate C.
D) “confident” means self-assured, which is the opposite of being uncertain. Eliminate D.


This means A is the correct answer.

questions.subject: verbal
